accepted.reason: Longest-common-substring/subsequence dynamic programming are high-value algorithmic kernels for optimization tasks.
Quaternion multiply/normalize operations are dense numeric primitives with clear performance relevance.
Dense/sparse binary conversion loops are compact data-processing kernels useful for Cython speedup training.
Square matrix multiplication is a canonical nested-loop compute workload with strong optimization signal.
Sparse-indexed feature aggregation over fixed-width blocks is loop-heavy and suitable for optimization-focused examples.

skipped.reason: Network address wrapper/properties are integration-layer code, not standalone compute kernels.
Event-loop handle lifecycle management is systems glue and not a direct optimization benchmark target.
Regex/XML scraping is parser glue with mixed IO concerns and lower transfer for Cython compute optimization.
Protocol stack scaffold is mostly unfinished state-machine/spec scaffolding, not a focused numerical kernel.
Project-coupled blackbox trainer has heavy external dependencies and weak portability as unpaired GRPO optimization data.
